rationale: The citing paper uses Dhar and Wertenbroch (2012) repeatedly in its conceptual framing to establish the dominant view that indulgent choices signal succumbing to temptation and weak self-control. That prior view is used as a core backdrop for the paper's main contribution, which is to show a different interpersonal signal (greater warmth) in public social-media settings. The citation is more than a passing literature-list mention because it helps motivate hypotheses and define the theoretical contrast the new work addresses.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Dhar and Wertenbroch (2012) as a key conceptual baseline on indulgence as temptation and negative self-control signaling, then builds its contribution by introducing a contrasting warmth-signaling effect in public sharing contexts.
